title: Leader Kalaignar's Revised Election Campaign Tour!
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the source's argument, political directness, rhetorical questions, repetition, irony, factual detail, and paragraph order. Names, dates, figures, quotations, and intentional English expressions are retained. Where Tamil idiom cannot be reproduced literally without sounding unnatural, the English follows its sense and rhetorical force without adding claims absent from the source. The original Tamil is reproduced in full below the translation and remains the authoritative text. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement. The source scan contains a five-date revised itinerary above the salutation, but that entire schedule is absent from the canonical OCR chapter text. It is restored in both the English translation and the Tamil reading copy below from source pages 368-369. The canonical Tamil source remains unchanged.
paragraphs: **3 May 2016, evening:** Madurai public meeting **5 May 2016, evening:** Chennai public meeting **8 May 2016, evening:** Thanjavur Manikoondu public meeting **11 May 2016:** Tiruvarur constituency, meeting voters from the campaign van **14 May 2016, 3:00 p.m.:** Chennai Chepauk public meeting

The Headquarters has today released my revised programme for campaigning in the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly election to be held on 16 May. Some Kazhagam district secretaries whose districts had been omitted from the tour programme first released had been pressing for campaign visits to their districts as well. Yet a few districts included in the original programme have now been omitted. The reason is my experience during the continuous six-day first stage of the tour.

All six days, the campaign van itself was my home! In every town, however, that van entered an ocean of humanity and emerged like a little child toddling and crawling forward, “taking tiny steps and stretching out its little hands.” You would have watched it on television with delight and pride!
Kazhagam treasurer and younger brother M.K. Stalin has said that he will extend his own tour further and asked that I should not strain myself too much.

On the advice of caring and concerned Udanpirappugal, my tour has now been “revised,” and this programme has been released. The names of some towns included earlier may now be missing. I give you my assurance that, after the Kazhagam alliance wins the coming general election, I shall certainly visit those omitted towns in person and share the joy of your victory.

With the feeling that I am united with you and you are united with me, consider my absence as though I had come in person. I lovingly ask every one of you to work so that the candidates of the Kazhagam alliance secure a magnificent victory in this election.
